title: WebGL
abstract: WebGL is a web platform technology for rendering interactive 2D and 3D graphics in compatible browsers using GPU acceleration.
description: A technology record for browser graphics and shader-based visual systems.
roleInEcosystem: WebGL supports procedural graphics, shader experiments, real-time visuals and interactive cultural interfaces.
bodyHtml:
Role

WebGL makes GPU-accelerated graphics available on the web. It is relevant to shaders, procedural visual systems and interactive public artefacts.

Use

Electronic Artefacts can use WebGL for generative visuals, motion systems and browser-native spatial interfaces.

References

See Khronos WebGL resources.
